paavan_purani.name: Paavan Purani
paavan_purani.summary:
A Texas competitor, Paavan Purani is on an upward trajectory, currently competing at the U17 level, with 17 entries across 7 tournaments. Every tournament adds experience, every match sharpens the game.

A true triple-threat, Paavan competes across singles, doubles, and mixed doubles. Competing across all disciplines builds a well-rounded game that pure specialists can't replicate.

Career highlights: Junior Nationals competitor (2 entries).

Paavan has competed at both U15 and U17, showing the adaptability needed to thrive as competition grows fiercer with each age group.

With 3 seasons of competitive experience, Paavan brings tournament-tested composure that only comes from years of stepping onto the court against the best.

paige_gu.name: Paige Gu
paige_gu.summary: Paige Gu has stepped onto the competitive stage with 1 tournament entry. Every champion's story has a beginning, and this is Paige's.

paige_xu.name: Paige Xu
paige_xu.summary:
Out of Illinois, Paige Xu has stepped onto the championship stage, currently competing at the U17 level, reaching the finals 2 times. With 35 entries across 12 tournaments, Paige is proving the ability to compete at the highest level.

Paige is known for playing up, regularly entering higher age groups and testing against older, more experienced opponents (4 tournaments with multi-age-group entries). That willingness to seek out tougher competition accelerates development in ways that playing it safe never can.

Paige is that rare three-discipline competitor — singles, doubles, and mixed — boasting a singles best of #4, a doubles best of #2, a mixed doubles best of #4. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: Junior Nationals competitor (6 entries).

Paige has shown remarkable doubles adaptability, partnering with 13 different players:
  • Eric Zhun Hu — a trusted XD partner (6 events, best: #5)
  • Sahana Samarthy — a trusted GD partner (4 events, best: #2)
  • Janelle Zhou — 3 events in GD, best: #3

A veteran of the junior circuit, Paige has competed across U13, U15 and U17, accumulating invaluable experience at every level. That depth of competitive history is an asset younger opponents simply cannot replicate.

With 4 seasons of competitive experience, Paige brings tournament-tested composure that only comes from years of stepping onto the court against the best.

phillip_you.name: Phillip You
phillip_you.summary:
A California competitor, Phillip You is a U.S. Junior National Champion — crowned at the biggest event on the domestic calendar in BD U13. That title represents the culmination of grueling competition against the very best in the country. With 5 career victories across 23 tournaments, Phillip has proven the ability to deliver when it matters most.

Phillip is known for playing up, regularly entering higher age groups and testing against older, more experienced opponents (4 tournaments with multi-age-group entries). That willingness to seek out tougher competition accelerates development in ways that playing it safe never can.

A true triple-threat, Phillip competes across singles, doubles, and mixed doubles — boasting 1 singles title, 3 doubles titles, 1 mixed doubles title. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: Junior Nationals champion (BD U13) • 1 ORC title.

Phillip has shown remarkable doubles adaptability, partnering with 17 different players:
  • Felicia Ho — a trusted XD partner (8 events, best: #2)
  • Kayden Hossain — a trusted BD partner (4 events, best: #2)
  • Jacob Zhou — 4 events in BD, producing 2 titles together

Having moved up to U17, Phillip is navigating tougher competition at the higher age group. The adjustment is natural — and with the pedigree Phillip brings, a return to the top is a matter of when, not if.

Phillip has been on this journey through U11, U13, U15, U17 — a multi-age-group career that few can match, accumulating invaluable experience at every level. That depth of competitive history is an asset younger opponents simply cannot replicate.

Phillip has competed across 5 different regions (NE, NW, NorCal, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

With 7 seeded entries across the career, Phillip consistently earns the respect of tournament organizers.

Over 5 seasons and 5 titles, Phillip has built a legacy that speaks volumes about what sustained effort and competitive fire can achieve.

Out of North Carolina, Pradyun Sai Rasamsetti has shown real competitive teeth, currently competing at the U19 level, breaking into the semifinals 1 time. Across 3 entries in 2 tournaments, every deep run is a signal of growing strength.

Pradyun competes in both singles and doubles, developing the kind of well-rounded game that pays dividends as competition intensifies at higher levels.

pragathi_maadawada.name: Pragathi Maadawada
pragathi_maadawada.summary:
Representing Texas, Pragathi Maadawada is a consistent semifinalist, currently competing at the U15 level, reaching the final four 4 times. That kind of reliability at the top of the draw is the signature of a player who will soon be contending for titles.

A true triple-threat, Pragathi competes across singles, doubles, and mixed doubles — boasting a singles best of #4, a doubles best of #3, a mixed doubles best of #3. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Pragathi has competed at both U13 and U15, showing the adaptability needed to thrive as competition grows fiercer with each age group.

Now in the 2nd season of competition, Pragathi's journey is gathering momentum with every tournament. The future is bright.

pragna_prakash.summary:

Pragna competes in both singles and doubles, developing the kind of well-rounded game that pays dividends as competition intensifies at higher levels.

prajwal_avadhani.name: Prajwal Avadhani
prajwal_avadhani.summary:
Representing California, Prajwal Avadhani stands as an ORC champion, currently competing at the U19 level, proving the ability to triumph against the strongest regional fields. Open Regional Championships draw the top talent from across the region, and taking home the title is a genuine achievement.

What makes Prajwal especially formidable is the versatility across all three disciplines — boasting a singles best of #3, 2 doubles titles, a mixed doubles best of #2. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: U.S. Selection Event participant (9 entries) • Junior Nationals competitor (11 entries) • 2 ORC titles.

Prajwal has shown remarkable doubles adaptability, partnering with 17 different players:
  • Uddhav Kasarabada — 11 events, 1 title together in BD/U15/U17
  • Manas Chintala — a trusted BD partner (9 events, best: #2)
  • Vignesh Jami — 4 events, 1 title together in BD

The 2025-2026 season tells a story of acceleration, with 1 title across 3 tournaments — a clear sign that Prajwal is still ascending.

A veteran of the junior circuit, Prajwal has competed across U13, U15, U17 and U19, accumulating invaluable experience at every level. That depth of competitive history is an asset younger opponents simply cannot replicate.

Prajwal has competed across 6 different regions (Midwest, NE, NW, NorCal, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

With 14 seeded entries across the career, Prajwal consistently earns the respect of tournament organizers.

With 6 seasons of competitive experience and 2 titles, Prajwal is a battle-hardened competitor whose journey is a testament to dedication, talent, and love for the game.

pranathi_chilukuri.name: Pranathi Chilukuri
pranathi_chilukuri.summary:
A California competitor, Pranathi Chilukuri is on an upward trajectory, currently competing at the U15 level, with 10 entries across 4 tournaments. Every tournament adds experience, every match sharpens the game.

A true triple-threat, Pranathi competes across singles, doubles, and mixed doubles. Competing across all disciplines builds a well-rounded game that pure specialists can't replicate.

Career highlights: Junior Nationals competitor (6 entries).

Now in the 2nd season of competition, Pranathi's journey is gathering momentum with every tournament. The future is bright.

pranav_gyajangi.name: Pranav Gyajangi
pranav_gyajangi.summary:
A Illinois competitor, Pranav Gyajangi is a multi-time champion on the junior circuit, currently competing at the U17 level, capturing 4 titles across 27 tournaments and 73 entries. Each win is earned through bracket battles against hungry opponents — and Pranav keeps finding ways to come out on top.

Pranav is known for playing up, regularly entering higher age groups and testing against older, more experienced opponents (5 tournaments with multi-age-group entries). That willingness to seek out tougher competition accelerates development in ways that playing it safe never can.

Pranav is that rare three-discipline competitor — singles, doubles, and mixed — boasting 1 singles title, 1 doubles title, 2 mixed doubles titles. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: U.S. Selection Event participant (10 entries) • Junior Nationals competitor (3 entries).

Pranav has shown remarkable doubles adaptability, partnering with 25 different players:
  • Erica Chen Lin — 7 events in XD, producing 2 titles together
  • Aarav Gupta — a trusted BD partner (6 events, best: #5)

The 2025-2026 season has been a step up, with 2 titles across 2 tournaments — a clear sign that Pranav is still ascending.

Pranav has been on this journey through U11, U13, U15, U17 — a multi-age-group career that few can match, accumulating invaluable experience at every level. That depth of competitive history is an asset younger opponents simply cannot replicate.

Pranav has competed across 5 different regions (Midwest, NE, NorCal, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

With 5 seasons of competitive experience and 4 titles, Pranav is a battle-hardened competitor whose journey is a testament to dedication, talent, and love for the game.

Pranav is peaking at the right time in 2025-2026, with 1 title across 5 tournaments — a clear sign that Pranav is still ascending.

Pranav has competed at both U11 and U13, showing the adaptability needed to thrive as competition grows fiercer with each age group.

Now in the 2nd season of competition, Pranav's journey is gathering momentum with every tournament. The future is bright.

Open Regional Championships are a significant step up from local events, and Pranav's results show the readiness for top-tier competition.

Notably, Pranav has entered higher age groups at 1 tournament, voluntarily taking on older competition — a bold move that reveals both confidence and a hunger for growth.

Pranav is that rare three-discipline competitor — singles, doubles, and mixed — boasting a singles best of #3. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

  • Ellie Chen — a trusted XD partner (4 events)

Pranav has competed at both U11 and U13, showing the adaptability needed to thrive as competition grows fiercer with each age group.

With tournaments spanning NE, NorCal, SoCal, Pranav isn't afraid to compete outside the home region — and that exposure to different playing styles only sharpens the game.

With 4 seasons of competitive experience, Pranav brings tournament-tested composure that only comes from years of stepping onto the court against the best.

ORCs are the highest level of regular-season competition, drawing the strongest fields in each region. Winning at this level repeatedly marks Prarthana as one of the premier players on the circuit.

Notably, Prarthana has entered higher age groups at 1 tournament, voluntarily taking on older competition — a bold move that reveals both confidence and a hunger for growth.

Prarthana is that rare three-discipline competitor — singles, doubles, and mixed — boasting 2 singles titles, 2 doubles titles, 3 mixed doubles titles. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: Junior Nationals competitor (3 entries) • 3 ORC titles.

Prarthana has shown remarkable doubles adaptability, partnering with 14 different players:
  • Arudh Sundar — 7 events, 1 title together in XD
  • Thea Yuan — 2 events in GD, producing 2 titles together

The 2025-2026 season tells a story of acceleration, with 7 titles across 5 tournaments — a clear sign that Prarthana is still ascending.

Prarthana has competed at both U11 and U13, showing the adaptability needed to thrive as competition grows fiercer with each age group.

pruthvi_chirravuri.name: Pruthvi Chirravuri
pruthvi_chirravuri.summary: Hailing from Texas, Pruthvi Chirravuri has stepped onto the competitive stage with 1 tournament entry. Every champion's story has a beginning, and this is Pruthvi's.

puneeth_gurrala.name: Puneeth Gurrala
puneeth_gurrala.summary: Puneeth Gurrala is just getting started on the junior circuit with 1 tournament entry. Every champion's story has a beginning, and this is Puneeth's.

puneeth_kumar_anumala.name: Puneeth Kumar Anumala
puneeth_kumar_anumala.summary:
Representing Texas, Puneeth Kumar Anumala is a multi-time champion on the junior circuit, currently competing at the U17 level, capturing 2 titles across 7 tournaments and 17 entries. Each win is earned through bracket battles against hungry opponents — and Puneeth keeps finding ways to come out on top.

A true triple-threat, Puneeth competes across singles, doubles, and mixed doubles — boasting 1 singles title, 1 doubles title. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Puneeth has shown remarkable doubles adaptability, partnering with 7 different players:
  • Aryan R Cimala — 3 events in BD, best: #3

The 2022-2023 season has been a step up, with 2 titles across 6 tournaments — a clear sign that Puneeth is still ascending.

Puneeth has competed at both U15 and U17, showing the adaptability needed to thrive as competition grows fiercer with each age group.

Puneeth has competed across 4 different regions (Midwest, NE, NorCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

Now in the 2nd season of competition, Puneeth's journey is gathering momentum with every tournament. The future is bright.

purab_narayani.name: Purab Narayani
purab_narayani.summary:
A North Carolina competitor, Purab Narayani is steadily building a competitive resume, currently competing at the U15 level, with 8 entries across 5 tournaments. Every tournament adds experience, every match sharpens the game.

Purab competes in both singles and doubles, developing the kind of well-rounded game that pays dividends as competition intensifies at higher levels.
